meta.title: The GigaPDF engine — 100% in-house, zero third-party PDF library
meta.description: Discover the GigaPDF in-house PDF engine: low-level read/write, real content editing, high-fidelity rendering, forms, digital signing, OCR and Office conversion. Compiled to WebAssembly, with no third-party PDF, Office or image library.

hero.title: A complete PDF engine,
hero.description: Most PDF tools stitch together third-party building blocks. GigaPDF took the opposite path: a full PDF engine, designed and written in-house, that reads, writes, edits, renders, signs and converts your documents. 100% in-house, no third-party PDF, Office or image library — that is what makes faithful editing and sovereign processing possible.

intro.label: In short
intro.title: Why an in-house engine changes everything
intro.description: An end-to-end PDF engine isn't a technical detail: it's what lets you edit a PDF like a Word document without breaking its layout, guarantee your files never leave our servers, and evolve every capability without depending on a third party's schedule.

core.label: Foundations
core.title: A 100% in-house engine, zero third-party PDF library
core.description: At the heart of GigaPDF sits a PDF engine written entirely in-house and compiled to WebAssembly to run at full speed on the server. It manipulates the PDF format at its lowest level, never delegating to a third-party PDF library.

points.lowLevel: Low-level PDF reading and writing: cross-reference tables (classic xref and xref streams), compressed objects (ObjStm), and recovery of damaged files.
points.flate: Fully in-house Flate compression and decompression: the document's streams are decoded and re-encoded with no external dependency.
points.wasm: Compiled to WebAssembly: native-grade performance, web portability, and a codebase we fully control.
points.souverain: No third-party PDF, Office or image library: the engine handles the format itself. Cryptography (the audited RustCrypto crates) and the JavaScript engine (Boa) deliberately rely on proven building blocks rather than risky in-house code — a security-first choice.

edit.label: Editing
edit.title: Truly edit the content, not cover it up
edit.description: Editing a PDF isn't slapping a white sticker over the text. GigaPDF genuinely rewrites the page's content stream to delete, move or replace text, shapes and images — exactly like a word processor.

points.realEdit: Real editing of text, shapes and images: adding, moving, resizing, rotating and reformatting directly inside the page content.
points.inPlace: In-place, lossless transforms: every element — text, image or shape — is moved or scaled via an affine transform, with no re-compression of images and no re-drawing of vectors.
points.restyle: Vector shape restyling: fill, stroke colour, stroke width and dash pattern edited and written back into the page.
points.tables: Editable tables: insert or delete rows and columns, merge cells, set borders and shading — including tables reconstructed from an existing PDF.
points.lists: Bulleted and numbered lists: indent levels, markers and numbering handled natively.
points.opacity: Per-element opacity and transparency, applied through the PDF's own graphics state (ExtGState).
points.stacking: Stacking order written into the document: bring to front / send to back is preserved when the file is reopened.
points.trueDelete: True object deletion: erased content disappears from the file, not hidden under a mask — no ghost data remains.
points.redaction: Evidence-grade redaction: redacted text and images are removed from the document irreversibly.
points.fidelity: Original layout preserved: changes blend into the document without breaking its structure.

pages.label: Pages & assembly
pages.title: Assemble, reorder and enrich pages
pages.description: Beyond content, the engine manipulates the document's structure: pages, links, outline and metadata are all handled natively, with no external tool.

points.rotate: Rotate, resize, move, duplicate, insert or delete any page.
points.assemble: Merge several PDFs and extract a selection of pages into a new document.
points.headers: Word-style headers and footers with automatic page numbering, baked into the layout.
points.links: Hyperlinks (URLs and internal navigation), bookmarks and outline reconstructed and preserved.
points.metadata: Document metadata — title, author, subject, keywords — read and editable.

render.label: Rendering
render.title: High-fidelity rasterization and rendering
render.description: To show a preview, generate a thumbnail or produce an image of a page, the engine draws every stroke, every curve and every glyph itself — a complete vector rasterizer, built in-house.

points.vector: Faithful vector rendering: paths, curves, fills and gradients are drawn at the resolution you need.
points.glyphs: Rendering of embedded font glyphs (TrueType and CFF): text appears exactly as in the original document.
points.selective: Selective rasterization: a page can be rendered without chosen elements — for example without its text — so real, editable content can be overlaid on a pixel-perfect background.
points.previews: High-definition previews and thumbnails for every page, at any scale.
points.images: Crisp page-image export, with no loss of quality and no artifacts.

forms.label: Forms
forms.title: Interactive forms, filled and flattened
forms.description: The engine understands interactive PDF forms: it reads their fields, pre-fills them and can freeze them permanently to produce a non-editable document.

points.types: Four field types supported: text fields, checkboxes, radio buttons and dropdown lists / combos.
points.fill: Programmatic filling of fields and reading of existing values.
points.flatten: Flattening: fields become fixed content, for a final, consistent document.
points.fidelity: Field appearance and positioning respected, compliant with the PDF standard.

annotations.label: Annotations
annotations.title: Natively annotate your documents
annotations.description: Highlight a passage, add a note, apply a stamp: the engine handles native PDF annotations, readable in any compliant viewer.

points.markup: Highlighting, underlining and text markup anchored in the right place, even on a rotated page.
points.notes: Notes and comments embedded in the document.
points.stamps: Stamps and arrows to validate, flag or draw attention.
points.standard: Standard-compliant annotations: openable and editable in any PDF viewer that follows the spec.

fonts.label: Fonts
fonts.title: Faithful fonts, automatically
fonts.description: A PDF's fidelity rests on its fonts. The engine identifies, fetches and embeds them itself, so your documents render everywhere exactly as intended — no tofu, no missing characters.

points.identify: Precise identification of the fonts used in each document.
points.download: Automatic download of missing fonts from the Google Fonts library, on the fly.
points.embed: Font embedding on save: the produced file is self-contained and renders faithfully everywhere.
points.cmap: Full support for composite fonts (Type0 / CID) and character mapping tables (CMap), for text extraction with no lost characters.

security.label: Security
security.title: Encryption, signing and archiving
security.description: Protect a document, prove its integrity, keep it over time: the engine handles encryption, digital signing and archival compliance end to end.

points.encryption: AES-256 and AES-128 document encryption (with backward-compatible reading of RC4-protected PDFs), built on the audited RustCrypto crates: password protection and permission control.
points.signature: PKCS#7 digital signing with a certificate: a verifiable proof of integrity, the way major PDF viewers do it.
points.tamper: Any tampering with a signed document is detectable — trust is demonstrable.
points.archive: Long-term archival compliance (PDF/A) to preserve your documents over time.

html.label: HTML → PDF
html.title: From HTML/CSS to PDF, natively
html.description: Generating a PDF from a web page or an HTML template is a common need. GigaPDF includes its own HTML/CSS layout engine — and the Boa JavaScript engine for dynamic pages.

points.css: In-house HTML/CSS rendering engine: style cascade, block and inline layout, tables, flexbox and pagination.
points.fonts: Web fonts embedded in the generated PDF, for pixel-faithful rendering.
points.js: Built-in Boa JavaScript engine (ES2021+): HTML templates that run scripts are rendered correctly, with no browser.
points.noThirdParty: All of it without a third-party browser or rendering engine: the full chain is in-house.

convert.label: Conversion
convert.title: Convert to truly editable documents
convert.description: The in-house conversion engine doesn't just take a picture of the page: it rebuilds genuinely editable documents, with selectable text, tables and repositioned images.

points.office: Conversion between PDF and office formats: Word (DOCX), Excel (XLSX), PowerPoint (PPTX) and OpenDocument (ODT, ODS, ODP).
points.formats: Import RTF, HTML, Markdown, CSV and plain text, plus JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP and AVIF images — all converted to PDF by the engine.
points.editable: Genuinely editable elements in the output — text, tables, images — not a flat scan.
points.web: Export to HTML and plain text to reuse the content elsewhere.
points.fidelity: Structure and formatting preserved as faithfully as possible from one format to another.

ocr.label: OCR
ocr.title: Make your scans searchable
ocr.description: A scanned PDF is just an image: you can neither search it nor copy its text. The in-house OCR, running server-side on our own infrastructure, recognizes the printed characters and adds an invisible text layer over the image, without changing its appearance.

points.recognize: Character recognition directly on scanned pages.
points.serverSide: OCR and text extraction are processed server-side, on our own engine: no third-party recognition service is involved.
points.layer: Invisible text layer overlaid on the image: the document looks identical to the eye, but becomes searchable.
points.searchable: Full-text search and copy-paste on documents that were only images.
points.preserve: The original image is fully preserved — OCR never alters the rendering.

privacy.label: Sovereignty
privacy.title: Free, open source and privacy-respecting
privacy.description: Because all processing runs on our own engine, your documents are never sent to a third-party service. OCR, text extraction and semantic search run on our own infrastructure: no external AI or OCR provider is ever involved.

points.free: Every engine capability is available in the free plan, with no watermark and no crippled version.
points.oss: Source-available code under the PolyForm Noncommercial license: audit it, study it, contribute.
points.serverSide: OCR and text extraction are processed server-side on our own engine: only the extracted text is then stored, to make your documents searchable.
points.localAi: Semantic search powered by fastembed locally: multilingual embeddings computed offline on our own infrastructure, with no third-party AI API.
points.noThirdParty: No external AI or OCR provider: processing stays end to end under our in-house engine.
points.selfHost: Self-hostable: install GigaPDF on your own servers to keep your documents at home.

ocrSection.label: OCR · Coverage
ocrSection.title: Strong recognition across many scripts
ocrSection.description: Our OCR is built on the state-of-the-art PaddleOCR models (PP-OCRv3/v4), run through an in-house runtime server-side — with no Tesseract binary and no third-party recognition service. Printed: Latin (English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese…), Cyrillic (Russian, Ukrainian…), Arabic, Hebrew (a model we trained ourselves), Devanagari (Hindi, Marathi…), Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Japanese and Korean. Handwriting, on demand: Latin (French included), Cyrillic and Greek.

results.label: Supported scripts
results.title: Scripts we recognize
results.lead: Thirteen printed-text recognizers — twelve PaddleOCR scripts plus a Hebrew model we trained ourselves — rounded out by handwriting on demand.

latin.languages: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese…

cjk.languages: Simplified and Traditional Chinese, Japanese, Korean

results.note: Honest framing: a dependency-free OCR engine built on the state-of-the-art PaddleOCR models, running server-side on our own infrastructure. It also reads Latin (French included), Cyrillic and Greek handwriting on demand (never auto-detected); other scripts remain limited to printed text.

langs.label: Language coverage
langs.title: Supported languages and scripts
langs.description: A single model family covering the world's major scripts, for printed text.

chips.latinLangs: FR · EN · DE · ES · PT · IT · PL · CS · TR · VI
chips.handwriting: Latin (FR…), Cyrillic, Greek handwriting — on demand

restore.label: Restoration front-end
restore.title: Your degraded scans, restored automatically
restore.description: Before recognition even starts, the engine crops, straightens and brightens damaged documents — photos, receipts, skewed or shadowed scans become legible, with no action on your part. A dedicated "photo / degraded" model variant takes over on the hard cases.

steps.autocrop: Automatic perspective crop: the engine detects the document quadrilateral (a photographed page, a receipt on a desk) and rectifies it head-on via homography — no more pages shot at an angle.
steps.illumination: Illumination correction (flat-field): uneven lighting and shadows from phone photos are flattened, while already-clean scans are left untouched.
steps.deskew: Fine deskew (up to ±5.7°): pages still slightly tilted are straightened back up.
steps.despeckle: Despeckle: grain and specks from low-end scans are wiped out.
steps.binarize: Adaptive Sauvola binarization: text is cleanly separated from the background, even under uneven lighting.
steps.model: Dedicated "photo / degraded" model variant for the toughest cases.

hero.title: A PDF editor that is
hero.description: Every line of GigaPDF is public, auditable and self-hostable. No black box, no lock-in: the cloud version and the one you host run the exact same code base.

pillars.label: Why open source

stack.label: Stack
stack.title: A modern, auditable stack

editing.label: Editing
editing.title: Edit every element. The layout doesn't move.
editing.description: A WYSIWYG editor that works inside the PDF, not on top of it: text, images and shapes are all editable in place — you click, you change it, the document stays pixel-faithful.

points.wysiwyg: Direct visual editing of text, images and shapes: move, resize, duplicate or delete any element in place, losslessly
points.fonts: Faithful fonts: originals are identified, fetched from Google Fonts and embedded automatically
points.formatting: Real formatting — bold, italic, underline, alignment, size and color
points.shapeStyling: Restyle vector shapes: fill, stroke color, stroke width and dash pattern
points.tables: Editable tables — insert or delete rows and columns, merge cells; bulleted and numbered lists
points.opacity: Element opacity and transparency, baked into the PDF
points.stacking: Stacking order — bring to front or send to back, saved in the PDF itself
points.layers: Layers to organize text, images and shapes — create, rename, lock and hide, kept across sessions
points.multiselect: Multi-selection to move, align or delete several elements in one gesture

collaboration.label: Collaboration
collaboration.title: Several hands on the same proof.
collaboration.description: The canvas is shared live: everyone sees each other's cursors, selections and edits — no exports, no attachments.

points.live: Edits appear in real time on every participant's canvas
points.sharing: Share by link or e-mail, with view / comment / edit roles
points.versions: Full version history with one-click restore
points.activity: Activity feed: who changed what, and when

trust.label: Trust
trust.title: Documents that hold up.
trust.description: Electronic signature, encryption and archival formats: everything you need for binding, long-lived PDFs.

points.signature: PKCS#7 digital signature with your P12 certificate — processed in memory, never stored
points.encryption: AES-256 encryption for documents, at rest and in transit
points.pdfa: PDF/A export for long-term legal archiving
points.ocr: Built-in, fully in-house OCR: your printed scans become searchable and copyable — Latin (accents included), Cyrillic, Arabic, Hebrew, Indic scripts and CJK

formats.label: Formats
formats.title: Everything in. Everything out.
formats.description: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OpenDocument: many formats import to PDF — and the way back works too.

points.imports: Import Office and OpenDocument formats (DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, ODT, ODS, ODP), plus RTF, TXT, HTML, Markdown, CSV and images
points.exports: Export to DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, ODT, ODP, RTF, Markdown, EPUB — plus PNG, JPEG, HTML, text
points.compression: Smart compression: lighter files with no visible quality loss

ged.label: DMS
ged.title: Your document archive, in order.

points.folders: Folders and subfolders to structure your workspaces
points.search: Full-text search inside document contents — OCRed scans included
points.trash: Trash with restore: nothing gets lost by accident
points.thumbnails: Auto-generated thumbnails to recognize your pages at a glance

mission.title: Our mission
mission.description: GigaPDF's mission is to democratize PDF editing by offering a professional, accessible, and fully open source tool. We believe everyone should have access to quality tools for managing their PDF documents.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: GigaPDF is a 100% open source project, powered by an in-house PDF engine written in Rust and compiled to WebAssembly, with no third-party PDF, Office, or image library. The code is source-available under the PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 license: you can inspect the source code, contribute to the project, or self-host it for noncommercial use.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: At GigaPDF, we take the protection of your personal data very seriously. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information when you use our service.

processing.title: Privacy by design
processing.description: GigaPDF is built around an in-house PDF engine that processes your documents on our own infrastructure, without relying on any third-party service. Processing your documents' content is designed to stay private.

serverSideOcr.title: OCR and text extraction on our infrastructure
serverSideOcr.description: Optical character recognition (OCR) and text extraction run server-side, on our own infrastructure: the image or PDF is never sent to a third-party recognition service. The extracted text is then stored to make your documents searchable (full-text and semantic search), with the embeddings computed locally on our infrastructure.

localAi.title: Local semantic search, no third-party AI
localAi.description: Semantic search relies on embeddings computed locally on our own infrastructure (fastembed, a multilingual model run offline). No third-party artificial intelligence or OCR API is used: your documents are never sent to an external AI service for analysis.

noAiSubprocessor.title: No AI or OCR subprocessor
noAiSubprocessor.description: Because OCR and embeddings run on our own infrastructure, we use no third-party artificial intelligence or text recognition provider.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Everything to use, integrate and self-host GigaPDF — the open-source WYSIWYG PDF editor.

overview.title: Overview
overview.p1: GigaPDF is an open-source WYSIWYG PDF editor: true in-place editing of text, images and forms, a document management system (DMS), and real-time collaboration. The code is source-available under the PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 license (free for noncommercial use) and is fully self-hostable.
overview.p2: The cloud version (giga-pdf.com) and the self-hosted version share the exact same code base. Use it as-is, embed it into your product via the embed widget and REST API, or host it on your own infrastructure.

items.editText: Edit text directly inside the PDF while keeping the original font
items.addText: Add text with Google or standard fonts (bold, italic, color, rotation)
items.shapes: Draw vector shapes: rectangle, ellipse, polygon, line, SVG path
items.annotate: Annotate: highlight, underline, strikethrough, free text, stamp
items.forms: Create and fill forms (text, checkboxes, radio buttons, lists, signature), then flatten them
items.watermark: Apply a text watermark (7 positions, opacity, rotation)
items.redact: Redact permanently and irreversibly (text and pixels)
items.organizePages: Organize pages: rotate, reorder, delete, duplicate, extract, split, merge, resize
items.headerFooter: Add running headers and footers with page numbering
items.metadata: Read and edit metadata (title, author, keywords…)

items.toPdf: Convert to PDF: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OpenDocument, images (JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP/AVIF), HTML, web page, RTF, text
items.fromPdf: Convert from PDF: Word, PowerPoint, ODT, ODP, Excel, PNG images, PDF/A
items.universalMerge: Universal merge: any files converted then merged into a single PDF
items.htmlToPdf: Render HTML/CSS to PDF natively, without a browser

items.encrypt: Encrypt with AES-128 or AES-256 (user and owner passwords)
items.permissions: 8 granular permissions: printing, modification, copying, annotation, forms, extraction, assembly, high-quality printing
items.sign: Sign digitally (PKCS#7) with your P12/PFX certificate — verifiable in Adobe Reader

items.ocr: Multilingual OCR (Latin, Cyrillic, Arabic, Hebrew, Devanagari, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Chinese, Japanese, Korean) — in-house engine, no Tesseract
items.searchablePdf: Make a scan searchable with an invisible text layer
items.semanticSearch: Semantic search across your documents, by meaning (embeddings + pgvector)
items.fullTextSearch: Full-text search on document name and content

items.share: Sharing by email (read/write) and public links with expiration

items.realtime: Real-time collaborative editing: shared cursors, element locking, scene synchronization

items.megaMenu: Mega menu listing the 36 tools by category
items.tools: 36 dedicated PDF tools with help pages
items.editor: Visual editor: zoom, pan, continuous page view

items.apiKeys: API keys with scopes and rate limits

items.wasm: In-house PDF engine in Rust → WebAssembly
items.zeroDep: Zero third-party dependency: no LibreOffice, no Tesseract, no Chromium, no poppler
items.nativeFormats: Images (PNG/JPEG/GIF/WebP/AVIF) and HTML/CSS rendering handled natively
items.license: Source code available under the PolyForm Noncommercial license

selfhost.title: Self-hosting
selfhost.intro: GigaPDF is built for self-hosting from day one. Two methods are supported: Docker (recommended) or a native install. The self-hosted version runs the exact same code base as the cloud.
selfhost.envNote: Before starting, copy the example environment file and fill in the key variables:

envVars.title: Key environment variables (.env)
envVars.redis: Redis: REDIS_URL (cache, Celery broker, WebSocket queue)
envVars.secret: APP_SECRET_KEY: backend secret key (min. 32 chars — openssl rand -hex 32)
envVars.s3: S3: S3_ENDPOINT, S3_ACCESS_KEY, S3_SECRET_KEY, S3_BUCKET (document storage)
envVars.smtp: SMTP: MAIL_SERVER, MAIL_PORT, MAIL_USERNAME, MAIL_PASSWORD (auth emails)
envVars.stripe: Stripe (optional): STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET — leave empty to disable billing
envVars.legal: Legal notice (prod): NEXT_PUBLIC_LEGAL_* (the app refuses to start without these, except on localhost)

docker.title: Docker (recommended)
docker.description: The simplest path. The PDF engine (WebAssembly) is bundled into the application — no third-party system binary and no extra setup.

services.worker: celery-worker — async processing (OCR, export, Office conversions)
services.beat: celery-beat — scheduled tasks (trash purge, billing sync)
services.web: web — Next.js app + TypeScript PDF engine (:3000)

docker.bundledNote: The PDF engine (WebAssembly) handles Office and HTML → PDF conversions, OCR, font embedding, rasterization, signatures and encryption. No third-party system binary (LibreOffice, Chromium, Tesseract, fontforge, poppler) is required.
docker.migrateNote: After the first start (and after every update), apply the database migrations:

native.title: Native (bare-metal / VPS)
native.description: A direct install on a server, behind Nginx with systemd units. Gives fine-grained control over each service.
native.sysdepsNote: None. The PDF engine (WebAssembly) is self-contained: Office and HTML → PDF conversions, OCR, font embedding, rasterization, signatures and encryption are all built in. No third-party binary (LibreOffice, Chromium, Tesseract, fontforge, poppler) to install.
native.backendNote: The pip install -r requirements.txt step installs the backend dependencies (the Python equivalent of a dependency install — this project does NOT use Composer). Then alembic upgrade head creates the schema.
native.frontendNote: pnpm install installs the monorepo's Node dependencies, pnpm build compiles the packages and apps (Turbo orders internal packages first).
native.runNote: Each service runs in its own process (in production: systemd units): uvicorn serves the API, two Celery processes (worker + beat) handle async and scheduled work, and the Next.js standalone server serves the web app.

roles.worker: celery worker — OCR, export, Office conversions, heavy jobs
roles.beat: celery beat — scheduled tasks (30-day trash purge, Stripe plan sync)
roles.web: node server.js (Next.js standalone) — public site + dashboard + TypeScript PDF engine

nginx.title: Reverse proxy (Nginx)
nginx.description: Nginx routes requests to the right service. The versioned FastAPI backend is the enumerable side; Next.js is the safe default for /api/.
nginx.default: /api/ (default) → Next.js (auth, PDF engine /api/pdf/*, office, fonts, health)

migrate.title: Database migrations (required on every update)
migrate.description: After every git pull / update, re-run the Alembic migrations from the project root (venv activated), then verify the applied revision. v1.3.0 ships migration 018_free_doc_limit.
migrate.verifyNote: alembic current must report the latest revision (018_free_doc_limit for v1.3.0).

api.title: API & developers
api.intro: GigaPDF exposes a full REST API and an embed widget to integrate the editor into your own product.
api.authDescription: Two access modes are accepted: a JWT token (Authorization: Bearer <token>) for signed-in users, or an API key (X-API-Key) for server-to-server and embed integrations. API keys are created from your dashboard, scoped, and SHA-256 hashed at rest.
api.keysDescription: Generate and manage your API keys from the developers page in your dashboard. Configure allowed domains and scopes to enforce least privilege.
api.embedDescription: Embed the PDF editor into any website in a few lines (Vanilla JS, React, or CDN).
api.swagger: Try the endpoints directly in your browser with Swagger UI.
api.redoc: Clean, responsive reference with detailed request/response schemas (Redoc).
api.openapi: OpenAPI specification — generate clients in any language or import into Postman.
api.exampleDescription: Upload a PDF document with your API key or token.
api.sdkDescription: Beyond the REST API, the PDF engine itself is published as a standalone TypeScript library, @qrcommunication/gigapdf-lib. Zero-dependency (Rust → WebAssembly), it reads, edits, renders, signs (PKCS#12 · PAdES-B-T · PAdES-LTV), encrypts, OCRs and converts your PDFs ↔ Office/HTML/RTF/Markdown/CSV right inside your own Node or browser app — no GigaPDF server required.
api.sdkInstallNote: The .wasm ships inside the package and is loaded via GigaPdfEngine.loadDefault(). In Next.js standalone, add the package to serverExternalPackages and trace the .wasm with outputFileTracingIncludes. Installation, the full API and examples are documented in the SDK README.

embedSdk.title: Embed SDK
embedSdk.description: Embed the GigaPDF editor into any website with just a few lines of code. Available in Vanilla JS, React, and via CDN.

embed.title: Embed SDK
embed.description: Embed the GigaPDF PDF editor directly into your website or application. Your users can edit PDFs without leaving your platform.

step1.description: Generate an API key from your GigaPDF dashboard. Configure allowed domains to secure access.

step2.description: Install the npm package or include the CDN script in your page. Initialize the editor with your API key.

step3.title: Embedded editor
step3.description: The PDF editor renders in a secure iframe. Communicate via postMessage to save, export, and more.

npm.description: Install the package from the npm registry to use in your JavaScript/TypeScript project.

cdn.title: CDN Script (plain HTML)
cdn.description: The fastest method. Add the script and initialize the editor in a few lines.

esm.title: Vanilla JS / ES Modules
esm.description: For projects with a bundler (Vite, Webpack, etc.). Direct import with TypeScript support.

react.title: React Component
react.description: React component with ref to control the editor programmatically. Compatible with React 18+.
